title: comptonella drupacea
intro: Comptonella drupacea, also known as the 'Maytree', is a plant species native to Australia and New Guinea, characterized by its fragrant flowers and edible fruit. The medicinal properties of Comptonella drupacea include its ability to treat respiratory issues and skin conditions due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of the plant include its potential to alleviate symptoms of colds, fever, and skin irritations, as well as its use as a natural remedy for wounds and minor injuries. Herbal preparations of Comptonella drupacea include teas, infusions, and ointments, which can be made from its leaves, flowers, and fruit.
